title: Designing Event-Driven Systems
subtitle: Concepts and Patterns for Streaming Services
author: Ben Stopford
themes: The log as the backbone of event-driven systems
Event sourcing, CQRS, and when to apply them
Event schemas and compatibility over time
Building multi-team service ecosystems with shared logs
When events are overkill — and when they're essential
awards: O'Reilly Best Seller in Streaming & Messaging
Foreword by Sam Newman (Building Microservices author)
Cited as foundational reading for engineers at Confluent
excerpt: Ben Stopford explains how service-based architectures and Apache Kafka can help you build business-critical systems, covering Event Sourcing, CQRS, and multi-team architectures with event streams as a shared source of truth.
